recentEvaluations.rationale: The tweet is a product announcement/joke about Codex and does not mention limits, caps, resets, or availability returning after a rate limit.
recentEvaluations.tweetText:
Announcing Codex. 

A new product from OpenAI that moves beyond coding, into cooking. We were already cooking before, but now *you* can cook too ... with Codex. It is powered by the same technology as our other Codex products. You can just cook things. https://t.co/tMnH1nn1VX

recentEvaluations.rationale: The tweet explicitly says they are "resetting the usage limits for all plans," meaning rate/usage caps are available again now.
recentEvaluations.tweetText:
Our Codex dashboards are showing increased rate of users hitting rate limits and since we don't fully understand why I have made the cautious decision of resetting the usage limits for all plans. Enjoy.

I also wanted to celebrate us finding a pocket of fraudulent accounts that we banned and have helped us regain some compute. The fight against abuse never stops, but it's important to mark the moment and make it a little shared victory.

recentEvaluations.tweetText: Whaaaa. Only realized now and apparently our repo was public since 11 months ago and noone told us?!

Clearly proof you can just build things. Have fun

automationEvents.tweetText: A fun one: I combined it with a skill and automation to physically print a one-page update on public discourse around Codex that I can read together with my morning coffee.

automationEvents.rationale: The tweet is about Codex plugins/integrations and account access, not about rate limits, caps, or usage resetting.
automationEvents.tweetText:
Boom. Plugins! Codex now has access to most things it couldn’t reach before and all super easy behind the same with you use for your ChatGPT account.

I use this for *everything*, from managing my calendar, triaging incoming bugs, keeping up to date with what everyone is doing at the company and so much more
